summary:
A teenager presents symptoms consistent with Alport syndrome including sensorineural deafness, blood in urine starting early teens, high BP; these findings along with a positive family history strongly suggest diagnosing as such. Given his age at symptom onset matches typical presentation patterns described extensively across multiple medical sources reviewed. To summarize further:

The teenage boy exhibits classic signs like sensorineural hearing impairment, proteinuria beginning around puberty alongside elevated blood pressure—features seen frequently among those diagnosed with Alport Syndrome—and has noted hereditary pattern within their household supporting its identification there too. This condition is known precisely due to how it manifests similarly over many documented cases involving young patients sharing comparable profiles medically reported previously. Therefore, based upon all collected data indicating concurrence amongst various reliable references consulted about this specific set of indicators together present herein today concerning him specifically makes me confident concluding he likely suffers indeed from said disease entity named 'Alport'. Henceforth recommending immediate referral into specialist care focusing particularly toward managing potential complications associated therewith moving forward henceforward without delay whatsoever
